channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
See photos cardboard box

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Abandoned vehicle., 17th July
item.description:
Vehicle has been in situ for a number of months and the number plates have now been removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jay Gardens, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sounding fire alarm, 17th July
item.description:
Twice, I passed by this building on the morning of 16 July. I heard a fire alarm sounding from the second floor each time. The windows were shut. The building looked vacant. There was no fence or gate.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queens Drive, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In alley way behind shops on bellgrove road next to massage palour., 17th July
item.description:
Garden waste was left in several black bin bags now further household rubbish has been added. Encouraging more rubbish to be left. Very unsightly. With garden waste possible to cause wildlife to nest of desecrate area. The whole of Churchfield Road is looking disgraceful. Rubbish left near post box.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bellegrove Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste dumped on pavement, 17th July
item.description:
Obstruction

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Damage pavement trip hazard

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): The Avenue, London

item.title: On gates, 17th July

item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Fire Hydrant Marker damaged, 17th July
item.description:
The Fire Hydrant Market outside No 2 Melbourne Avenue is damaged with fascia detached.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Melbourne Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The street is incredibly dirty and smells foul, 17th July
item.description:
The pavements are very unclean, clearly are not being properly cleaned. I did bump into a gentleman doing the street cleaning but he was not doing much, he was slowly litter picking and not sweeping the streets. The street needs to be swept and by the smells of things actually washed. I don’t really understand why we can’t identify people within the complaints considering it was a council worker not really cleaning the space. How would we be able to address noticing this issue as part of the problem otherwise? I wouldn’t suggest it was his fault I don’t believe he was given enough tools or enough support staff either. The streets need to be cleaned they smell of urine very strongly and one gentleman litter picking doesn’t help this issue you see. A good few months the smell has been getting much stronger, there’s also a lot of gum and stickers on the floor directly outside of Primark and the bins nearby were overflowing.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hare Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped on street, 17th July
item.description:
Flytipped/dumped rubbish outside HMO, yet again.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gloucester Drive, Hackney, London

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37-47, Kimberley Drive, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London

item.description:
